# Pipeline status report — methodology (`260622_report.csv`)

**Date:** 2026-06-22
**Generated by:** `tools/generate_pipeline_report.py`
**Purpose:** Per-pathogen snapshot of the current pipeline, taken *before* a round of
improvements, so the same script can be re-run later to measure the effect of changes.

One row per pathogen (15 codes from `config/pathogens.csv`). The script is read-only over
`output/` and `config/`; re-run with `python tools/generate_pipeline_report.py [out.csv]`.

## Key definitions (decided with the team)

- **Assay counting unit** — each `(assay_id, activity_type, unit)` row in
  `18_assays_master.csv`. An assay measured two ways (e.g. MIC *and* inhibition-zone)
  counts as two. This matches how the pipeline curates, models and selects datasets.
- **Molecules** — taken from the cleaned activities `08_chembl_cleaned_data.csv.gz`
  (after QC/curation), deduplicated by `compound_chembl_id`.
- **Classification** — `target_type_curated_extra` (ORGANISM / SINGLE PROTEIN / DISCARDED),
  the post-curation collapse produced in step 12.
- **ORG actives / inactives** — from the binarized datasets in `12_datasets/` (`bin` column),
  taken at the **reference cutoff** (`reference_cutoff()` in `src/pathogen_utils.py`: positional
  middle cutoff, or the most lenient for `%` endpoints — the same convention steps 20/21 use).
  Only ORG assays that reached the dataset stage contribute. Decoys / non-assessed compounds
  are excluded by intersecting with the set of molecules actually assessed in ORG assays.
- **"Kept" set** — datasets with `selected == True` in `17_final_datasets.csv` (i.e. after the
  correlation-based deduplication in step 17). A molecule is "kept" if it appears in any
  selected dataset.

> **Join note:** `08_chembl_cleaned_data.csv.gz` keys assays by the numeric `assay_id`,
> whereas the master and dataset tables use the ChEMBL string id — the join uses
> `assay_chembl_id`.

## Columns

| column | definition |
|--------|------------|
| `pathogen_code` | code from `config/pathogens.csv` |
| `n_total_mols` | activity records in `08` cleaned data (molecules counted with duplicates) |
| `n_unique_mols` | distinct `compound_chembl_id` in cleaned data |
| `n_total_mols_org` | cleaned activity records whose `(assay,activity,unit)` is ORGANISM |
| `n_unique_mols_org` | distinct `compound_chembl_id` among those |
| `n_assays_org` | master rows classified ORGANISM |
| `n_assays_sp` | master rows classified SINGLE PROTEIN |
| `n_assays_discarded_class` | master rows classified DISCARDED (incl. a few uncurated NaN) |
| `n_unique_actives_org` | distinct molecules active (`bin==1`) in ≥1 ORG dataset, reference cutoff |
| `n_unique_inactives_org` | distinct ORG molecules never active (only `bin==0`) |
| `n_assays_a` | number of selected **A** datasets (1 assay each) |
| `n_assays_b` | number of selected **B** datasets (1 assay each) |
| `n_assays_m` | number of selected **M** (merged) datasets |
| `n_assays_underlying_m` | distinct assays pooled across the selected M datasets |
| `n_assays_not_kept` | ORG assays not retained in any A/B/M dataset (`n_assays_org − kept assays`) |
| `frac_discarded` | `n_assays_not_kept / n_assays_org` |
| `frac_unique_mols_discarded` | fraction of unique ORG molecules not present in any kept dataset |
| `frac_unique_actives_discarded` | fraction of unique ORG actives not present in any kept dataset |
| `n_strains` | distinct non-null `assay_strain_curated` among ORG assays |
| `n_assays_raw` | rows in `07_assays_raw.csv` (before any curation) |
| `n_assays_cleaned` | rows in `08_assays_cleaned.csv` (after QC); equals `org + sp + discarded_class` |
| `frac_unique_inactives_discarded` | fraction of unique ORG (pure) inactives not in any kept dataset |
| `mean_auroc_selected` | mean cross-validated AUROC over the selected datasets (NaN if none) |
| `pct_total_chem_space_covered` | `100 × kept unique ORG molecules / (unique compounds in 07_compound_counts)` — % of the **full** pathogen compound universe; same denominator as the step-21 coverage figure |
| `pct_org_chem_space_covered` | `100 × kept unique ORG molecules / n_unique_mols_org` — % of **all** ORG-assessed molecules (incl. qualitative-only and `none`); exact complement of `frac_unique_mols_discarded` (`= 100 × (1 − frac_unique_mols_discarded)`) |
| `pct_org_qtmx_chem_space_covered` | `100 × kept ORG mols (in qt/mx) / (unique mols in quantitative+mixed ORG assays)` — coverage over only the **coverable** universe, excluding qualitative-only molecules that A/B/M can never reach |

### ORG assay-size breakdown and qualitative-only pool

Computed from per-assay compound sets in `08` cleaned data (ORG only, deduplicated by
`compound_chembl_id`). For each bucket — small (`< 20` compounds) and qualitative-only
(`dataset_type == qualitative`) — six columns:

| suffix | meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `n_assays_org_<b>` | number of ORG assays in the bucket |
| `pct_assays_org_<b>` | as a % of all ORG assays |
| `mols_org_<b>` | unique molecules in the bucket (union across its assays) |
| `pct_chemspace_org_<b>` | those molecules as a % of `n_unique_mols_org` |
| `mols_org_excl_<b>` | molecules found **only** in this bucket |
| `pct_chemspace_org_excl_<b>` | exclusive molecules as a % of `n_unique_mols_org` |

Buckets `<b>` ∈ {`lt20`, `ql`}.

- `lt20` covers ORG assays with `< 20` compounds. `mols_org_excl_lt20` is the molecules found
  **only** in `< 20`-compound assays (never in any `>= 20`-compound assay) — i.e. chemical space
  that has no route into A/B and is hard to merge. (The `>= 20` complement is computed internally
  to find this exclusive set but is not reported, to keep the table compact.)
- For `ql`, the *exclusive* set (`mols_org_excl_ql`) is the molecules reachable **only** through
  qualitative ORG assays — i.e. the chemical space lost to A/B/M, since those routes consume
  quantitative/mixed data only. (Non-exclusive `ql` molecules also appear in quantitative/mixed
  assays and can still be captured there.)

## Naming change vs the original request

The original column list contained `n_assays_discarded` twice with two different meanings.
They were split:

- `n_assays_discarded_class` — assays classified neither SP nor ORG.
- `n_assays_not_kept` — ORG assays not retained as A/B/M.

## Consistency checks (hold for all 15 pathogens)

- `n_assays_org + n_assays_sp + n_assays_discarded_class == n_assays_cleaned`.
- `n_assays_a + n_assays_b + n_assays_underlying_m == n_assays_org − n_assays_not_kept`.
- `n_unique_actives_org + n_unique_inactives_org ≈ n_unique_mols_org`
  (small gap = ORG molecules whose assays never reached the binarizable dataset stage,
  e.g. `dataset_type == none`).

Cross-checked against `output/saureus/21_summary_table.xlsx` (Pipeline Funnel / By Condition):
ORG=27,542, SP=2,019, discarded=657, selected B=3 / M=75 — all match.

## Caveats

- ORG actives/inactives use the **reference** cutoff because non-selected assays have no
  "selected" cutoff; selected datasets may have been binarized at a different (best) cutoff.
  The discarded-fraction numerators intersect kept sets with the reference-cutoff totals so
  the fractions stay within `[0, 1]`.
- `campylobacter` has no selected datasets, so its `frac_*_discarded` are 1.0 and
  `mean_auroc_selected` is NaN.
